notes: Roman general known for his rapaciousness and involvement in military and political affairs during the 1st century CE.

docs.title: Life of Otho
docs.author: Plutarch
docs.excerpt: … Valens, the other general, was so rapacious, that neither what he plundered from enemies nor what he stole or got as gifts and bribes from his friends and allies could satisfy his wishes.

docs.title: Histories, Book 1
docs.author: Tacitus
docs.excerpt: This officer in the course of the following day entered the Colonia Agrippinensis with the cavalry of the legion and of the auxiliaries, and together with them saluted Vitellius as Emperor.
